Reconciled live Kansas inventory first and confirmed thirteen existing Kansas V2 routes before this add: kansas-river-lawrence-8th-eudora, kansas-river-eudora-de-soto, kansas-river-de-soto-edwardsville, kansas-river-junction-city-ogden, kansas-river-ogden-manhattan, kansas-river-manhattan-st-george, kansas-river-st-george-wamego, kansas-river-wamego-belvue, kansas-river-belvue-kaw-river-state-park, kansas-river-topeka-water-plant-seward, kansas-river-lecompton-lawrence-riverfront, kansas-river-turner-bridge-kaw-point, and kansas-river-seward-lecompton. Added Kansas River Edwardsville Access Ramp / River Front Park to Turner Bridge Access Ramp as kansas-river-edwardsville-turner-bridge. Qualification path: Friends of the Kaw documents Edwardsville at river mile 16.6 with GPS 39.05056, -94.81665, Turner Bridge at river mile 9.2 with GPS 39.09396, -94.71176, Turner Bridge as the next day-trip access downstream, and the WaterOne low-head dam at river mile 14.8 with a required river-left-only portage. The WaterOne page explicitly warns not to run the dam or river-right notch and describes a rough 100+ yard loose-rock carry; River Access Tips and Paddle FAQ provide hazard-check, public-river, public-ramp, 5,000 cfs novice, and 8,000 cfs all-paddler context. USACE Kansas River recreation material supports the low-side and broad optimal-impact bands, and USGS 06892350 Kansas River at De Soto is used as the conservative upstream lower-Kaw discharge proxy. Implemented two-sided 1,500-5,000 cfs ideal, 1,000 cfs too-low, and 8,000 cfs too-high with mixed-source strength. Caveats include mandatory WaterOne portage, no running the dam, no river-right notch, proxy-gauge limits, same-day visual checks, private banks, few sandbars, wind, high-water sandbar loss, low-water slow travel, and rough Turner Bridge parking. No route-gallery image was selected. Validation: ledger JSON parse passed; npm.cmd run typecheck passed with route-data audit for 202 routes; npm.cmd run build passed and generated /rivers/kansas-river-edwardsville-turner-bridge/; git diff --check passed with line-ending warnings only; npm.cmd test reran typecheck successfully then failed in the existing Vite/Vitest/esbuild sandbox config-loading path.

American Whitewater still supports the exact 5-mile Class II(III) route shape, Echo gauge relationship, hazards, Henefer put-in, Taggarts take-out, Croyden bridge/portage, Taggarts Falls, and current 360 cfs medium-runnable context. Shell USGS Water Services IV fetches for 10132000 and the already-live Green River 09234500 again failed unable to connect. The implementation blocker remains endpoint quality and access legitimacy: no official/manager/AW text source exposed exact coordinates for both endpoints, especially the Taggarts river-right take-out below Taggarts Falls. Utah DWR stream-access guidance remains current as of March 10, 2026 and makes lawful access, floating without stopping, private beds/banks, public access points, and the upper Weber special case first-class constraints. Utah WRI still describes the take-out as county-owned old highway off the Taggart Exit, not an officially established boat ramp, with limited/congested parking and unclear unified rules. Do not ship from inferred river-line coordinates, the supplemental Outbound coordinate, or a generic Taggarts locality.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only if source-backed coordinates are captured for the Henefer-Echo Angler Access WMA/main parking/launch plus the Taggarts river-right take-out below Taggarts Falls, and current access/parking legitimacy is confirmed by manager, county, AW map feature, official GIS/map data, or similarly strong access evidence. Also reconfirm USGS 10132000 through the product-fetchable Water Services path before shipping.

gauge.notes: USGS 10132000 Weber River at Echo is the direct gauge tied to the AW reach. During the 21:10 America/Chicago pass, AW displayed 360 cfs medium runnable, but the shell Water Services IV fetch for 10132000 failed unable to connect. Future implementation must confirm the product Water Services path from the app runtime before shipping.

thresholdSupport.notes: AW provides direct reach/gauge linkage and current runnable state. Recent AW trip reports suggest 205 cfs is low/scrapy and around 250 cfs is better for packrafts; a May 18, 2026 report described 644 cfs as engaging but noted the low-bridge portage and Taggarts flip risk. The route page warns around 600-700 cfs may make the Croyden bridge impassable and notes higher-flow holes at 1400+ cfs. The 21:10 pass showed 360 cfs as medium runnable. Use conservative whitewater scoring; do not overclaim a polished broad-audience ideal range without implementation review.

endpoints.putIn: Henefer-Echo Angler Access WMA / western Henefer Exit 112 main dirt parking access
endpoints.takeOut: Taggarts river-right take-out below Taggarts Falls / I-84 Exit 108 area
endpoints.coordinateSource: Coordinates remain unresolved. DWR confirms the Henefer-Echo Angler Access WMA as a public managed launch/parking area and AW/Weber State identify the route endpoints, but this run did not find source-backed exact coordinates for both implementation points. Utah WRI describes the Taggarts take-out area as county-owned old highway off the Taggart Exit, not an officially established boat ramp, so a generic map pin or supplemental Outbound coordinate is not enough.

candidates.notes: Strong route-shape and threshold lead, but still blocked as needs_manual_coordinates with access-precision concerns at Taggarts and a runtime Water Services fetch failure that should be rechecked before implementation. Treat as Class II(III) whitewater/swiftwater if later implemented, with rock garden, Taggarts Falls, Croyden bridge/portage, private-bank, legal-access, parking-fee, tubing-crowd, cold-water, and high-flow bridge/hole caveats.

gauge.notes: Implemented with USGS 09234500 Green River near Greendale, UT as the direct tailwater gauge immediately below Flaming Gorge Dam. USGS legacy current conditions showed same-day May 30, 2026 observations: 1,520 cfs discharge and 9.01 ft gage height at 07:30 MDT. Shell Water Services fetch was blocked in this environment, but the official USGS legacy page exposed current same-day values and the app uses the standard USGS provider path.

thresholdSupport.notes: Implemented as minimum-only at 1,000 cfs on USGS 09234500. American Whitewater ties the broader Flaming Gorge-to-Lodore reach to the Greendale gauge and showed the reach runnable at 1,000 cfs; Recreation.gov/Ashley support the exact 7-mile Section A route as Class I-II. No ideal range or high-water cutoff was claimed because the source package does not provide a clean Section A-specific two-sided band.

endpoints.putIn: Spillway Boat Launch Area below Flaming Gorge Dam
endpoints.coordinateSource: Ashley National Forest recreation pages publish coordinates for both Spillway Boat Launch Area and Little Hole Boat Launch Area.

candidates.notes: Added 2026-05-30 as the first Utah V2 route. Scoped strictly to Section A from Spillway Boat Launch to Little Hole, marked routeType whitewater, and written with cold tailwater, dam-release fluctuation, fee/pass, required gear, commercial dory traffic, busy ramp, no-camping, legal-access, and invasive-species caveats.

image.notes: USGS has a public-domain Spillway Boat Launch image by Anne Ballmann, but no file was downloaded into the route gallery during this run because the local shell could not fetch remote assets. Image audit records the future rights-clean lead.

candidates.route: Apple River Canyon State Park / Hanover gauge family
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-30 08:59 America/Chicago review. Apple River looked like a possible northwest Illinois whitewater/access lead because American Whitewater's Illinois gauge list includes USGS 05419000 Apple River near Hanover with current-looking flow/stage context, and IDNR confirms Apple River Canyon State Park is public state-park land along the river. The route was not added because no normal public put-in/take-out day-trip pair, official canoe/kayak access page, or manager/water-trail route shape surfaced. IDNR's Apple River Canyon page emphasizes hiking, fishing, hunting, camping, and picnicking rather than boating access, and the only strong current evidence found was gauge-level rather than route-level. Do not ship from gauge existence plus park adjacency in Illinois.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only if IDNR, a county/city/public water-trail manager, American Whitewater route page, or a strong local paddling authority publishes a specific Apple River paddling reach with public endpoints and numeric thresholds tied to USGS 05419000 or another product-fetchable gauge.

candidates.route: Freeport to Rockton / Stephenson County water-trail family
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-30 02:58 America/Chicago review. IDNR Public Waters lists the Pecatonica River, Illinois Paddling Council documents the Pecatonica River Water Trail and the Wes Block Landing as a developed Stephenson County/Friends of the Pecatonica River launch six river miles above Tutty's Crossing, and the Greater Freeport Partnership describes four friendly water-trail paddle trips from the Wisconsin state line to downtown Freeport with easy feet-dry take-outs, free parking, picnic facilities, and shuttle/blockage information. IllinoisPaddle's fact sheet names Freeport-to-Rockton access/mileage landmarks including Stephenson Street Park, Sumner Park Dam, and Harrison/Rt. 75. The route was not added because no official/AW/strong route-specific numeric paddling threshold tied to USGS 05435500 at Freeport or 05437050 near Shirland surfaced, and direct USGS Water Services IV fetches for both gauges failed from the automation shell. USGS visible monitoring pages confirm the gauge identities but did not expose enough current/sample detail through the browser text view for a product-live add.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry if Friends of the Pecatonica River, Stephenson County, a water-trail manager, AW, or a strong local route guide publishes numeric paddling guidance tied to USGS 05435500 or 05437050, and if product-fetchable current USGS observations can be verified.

candidates.route: N2300 Road to Vermilion River / Bailey Falls
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-30 08:28 America/Chicago review. American Whitewater documents Bailey Creek as a 0.5-to-1.5-mile I-II(IV) waterfall/runout route, ties it to the Vermilion River near Leonore gauge, and exposes current-looking Illinois index flow context. The route was not added because the public-access gate fails: AW says Bailey Creek is now part of IDNR properties but has not yet been officially cleared for public access. It also lacks a normal public put-in/take-out day-trip shape and would need explicit whitewater or park-and-play product handling before any future implementation.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only if IDNR, a site manager, or American Whitewater documents that Bailey Creek / Bailey Falls is officially open for public paddling access, with named legal access points and stable AW threshold guidance tied to the Leonore gauge.

candidates.route: Rockton Dam play-park lead
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-30 01:27 America/Chicago review. American Whitewater documents Rockton Dam as a 0.25-mile II-IV play-park reach with USGS 05437500 / Rock River at Rockton gauge context and detailed feature notes, but the same AW page warns that paddling above or down the dam is technically illegal and describes multiple severe keepy / nasty hydraulic hazards. This fails the conservative V2 public-access and same-day user-safety bar, even though the gauge and numeric feature notes are real.
candidates.retryCondition: Do not revisit for V2 route implementation unless the dam legality/access issue is formally resolved by the manager and a public, designated whitewater access/course replaces the current dam-play hazard story.

candidates.route: Duerr Forest Preserve to Fabyan Forest Preserve
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-30 01:27 America/Chicago review. Openlands documents Duerr Forest Preserve to Fabyan West Forest Preserve as an about-10-mile Fox River Water Trail trip for intermediate paddlers or beginners with an experienced guide, with St. Charles and Geneva dam portages, shallow water below dams, wind/sun caveats, and a USGS-check instruction. Fabulous Fox Water Trail lists Jon J. Duerr Forest Preserve and Fabyan Forest Preserve as signed public access sites with coordinates, amenities, and river miles. USGS 05551000 at South Elgin is a same-corridor official gauge lead. The route was not added because no official, AW, manager-published, or strong route-specific numeric low-water floor / ideal range / high-water cutoff tied to that gauge was found; generic USGS-check guidance and RiverScout-style community ranges are not enough for Illinois V2 scoring.

gauge.notes: The route uses USGS 05387440 Upper Iowa River at Bluffton, IA, a live same-river gauge in the Bluffton corridor. Miles Paddled ties the exact Chimney Rock Road-to-Malanaphy Springs route to this gauge with a recorded 4.27 ft / 375 cfs observation.

thresholdSupport.notes: Miles Paddled says more than 150 cfs on the Bluffton gauge is needed to avoid scraping and dragging on this exact route, and describes 375 cfs / 4.27 ft as lower than average but entirely adequate. Wisconsin River Trips corroborates broader Bluffton-corridor behavior by treating 150 cfs as scrapey, 200 cfs as low, 305 cfs as more than plenty, and roughly 700+ cfs as too pushy for the nearby Cattle Creek-to-Bluffton section. The app uses a conservative minimum-only 150 cfs floor because the strongest exact-route evidence does not publish a complete upper ceiling.

thresholdSupport.notes: Wisconsin River Trips publishes route-specific Fulton-gauge guidance for this Ozark Wildlife Area route: less than 150 cfs may be too shallow, 151-230 cfs is very shallow but likely viable for shallow-drafting boats, 231-300 cfs is average and good, 301-400 cfs is above average but still good, 401-550 cfs is higher but acceptable with poorer water quality, 551-700 cfs is high and likely less attractive, 701-1000 cfs is very high and likely advanced-only, and 1001+ cfs is expert-only. The app uses 231-400 cfs as the broad preferred window, 150 cfs as the low cutoff, and 701+ cfs as beyond a broad recreational recommendation.

endpoints.coordinateSource: Coordinates are from Jackson County Conservation's North Fork water-trail access table: Ozark Bridge at 42.19, -90.87 and Caven Bridge Access at 42.18, -90.83. The app treats them as access-area coordinates because the published table rounds to two decimals and WRT notes both landings are undeveloped, with a practical public pull-off/trail near 185th Street at the put-in and east-bank public use at the Caven/60th Ave take-out.

candidates.notes: Implemented 2026-05-25 as a fresh Iowa route after the prompted Iowa candidates were already implemented or deferred. candidates.route: Lenon Mill Park Access to Middle Raccoon River Access

candidates.notes: Implemented 2026-05-25 as a guarded Iowa route. This route clears the bar with a direct same-river live gauge, route-specific community threshold guidance, official public state-park/county access context, and explicit hazards. It is guarded because the Highway 410 launch is a bridge access inside a state park rather than a developed named ramp, and because many broad-audience paddlers may be better served by taking out at the Backbone Lake dam/ramp before the dam-to-Dundee strainers. Supplemental image pass downloaded and wired the public-domain USGS Backbone State Park asset; it is route-corridor/park context rather than an on-water Maquoketa main-channel photo.

candidates.route: Highway 83 to Upper Nemahbin Lake Public Boat Launch
candidates.blocker: The route has a direct same-river Delafield gauge and credible route-specific paddling notes, but the upstream access does not meet the current named-public-access bar. Miles Paddled describes Highway 83 as a workable but not ideal roadside bridge shoulder with traffic and no developed launch, and Wisconsin River Trips treats Highway 83 as a segment point rather than a public landing. The downstream Upper Nemahbin public boat launch is well supported by Wisconsin DNR, but that does not fix the put-in.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only if City of Delafield, Waukesha County, Wisconsin DNR, or another access manager documents a legal public Bark River launch at or near Highway 83, or if the route can be reshaped around two named public endpoints without creating a deadfall-heavy or portage-heavy trip.

aliases: Bark River III - Highway 83 to Upper Nemahbin Lake Public Boat Launch
Bark River - Highway 83 to Upper Nemahbin Lake

gauge.notes: The old route seed uses USGS 05426067, Bark River at Nagawicka Road at Delafield, and Miles Paddled notes the route passes that gauge shortly below Highway 83. USGS also lists a Bark River at Delafield monitoring location, but the access blocker stopped implementation before final gauge selection.

thresholdSupport.notes: Miles Paddled gives route-specific guidance that below about 50 cfs is too low for comfort, with 70+ cfs making the low pipeline below the Delafield dam more consequential. This is usable community guidance, but it cannot overcome the weak put-in.

endpoints.takeOut: Upper Nemahbin Lake Public Boat Launch

candidates.notes: Reviewed 2026-05-25 after the prompt's Bark/Sugar candidates were found already implemented. This is not ready for V2 because the first quality-bar requirement is not met: the put-in is plausible but not a named public access. The route also includes Lake Nagawicka wind/motorboat exposure, a downtown Delafield dam portage without a designated re-entry, a low pipeline in swift current, and potential deadfall near the put-in, so it should not be reshaped casually without better official access support.

candidates.route: Whitewater Drive / Fillmore area to Highway D61
candidates.blocker: Dubuque County confirms public canoe/kayak access context for Whitewater Creek, and Miles Paddled plus Wisconsin River Trips document the canyon route with numeric level guidance, but the only live gauge support found is USGS 05418400 North Fork Maquoketa River near Fulton. candidates.route: Dexter-Huron / Delhi Metropark day-trip family
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-29 18:09 America/Chicago recheck preserved the blocker. Metroparks, HRWC / Huron River Water Trail, Skip's, and Outdoor Michigan support a real Dexter-Huron-to-Delhi route with public launches, livery service, and Dexter-Huron launch coordinates. AW also provides numeric Delhi Mills bands on USGS 04174500, but AW warns the Ann Arbor gauge is below Barton and Argo dams and can diverge from the upstream Delhi Mills reach. The source explicitly requires visual confirmation and graph interpretation, so the proxy remains too weak for a new V2 route despite strong access evidence.

candidates.route: Bowman Bridge to Upper Branch Bridge
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-29 Michigan review. Rivers.gov and Recreation.gov strongly support the Pere Marquette National Scenic River corridor, Memorial-Day-to-Labor-Day watercraft permits, and the managed segment from Bowman Bridge to Upper Branch Bridge. USGS 04122500 at Scottville is a real lower-river gauge, but this pass found no credible route-specific numeric paddling floor, preferred range, or high-water cutoff tied to that gauge for the Bowman-to-Upper-Branch day segment. Public access is promising, but adding from permit segmentation plus gauge existence would overclaim level calibration.

candidates.route: Sturgeon Falls to NF2270 / FSR193
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-29 Michigan review. American Whitewater documents a 4.7-4.87 mile Sturgeon Gorge reach with USGS/Sidnaw gauge context and public take-out description, and Rivers.gov confirms the wild/scenic Sturgeon River context. The route was not added because AW explicitly says it has no awareness of best runnable flows and that listed ranges are tentative; the reach includes a dangerous waterfall approach with documented fatal consequence and Class II-III(V+) framing. This is outside the conservative day-route bar without stronger local/manager threshold and access guidance.

candidates.route: Eagle's Landing / W. Avon Road to E. Avon Road / Yates Dam
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-29 17:00 America/Chicago recheck preserved the blocker. Outdoor Michigan now directly lists Clinton River - Eagles Landing Boat Launch as a carry-in at 42.6645, -83.15393, and the 2024 CRWC map lists CRWC Office / Eagles Landing and Yates Cider Mill as Clinton River Water Trail launch assets. American Whitewater still gives the strongest threshold package for the 4.7-mile Class I(II) reach on USGS 04161000, including about 1.3 ft / 70 cfs minimum and about 3.0 ft / 500 cfs flood-stage caution. The route was not added because CRWC documents the Yates bypass channel redirecting river flow around Yates Dam, Oakland County describes active 2026 operations transition and river-restoration work at Yates Park / Clinton River Oaks, and the exact safe take-out/portage geometry remains unresolved in a historically lethal low-head-dam zone.

candidates.route: Hayes Arboretum Road to Test Road / Whitewater Gorge
candidates.blocker: Fresh 2026-05-30 20:39 America/Chicago review. American Whitewater documents the East Fork Whitewater River Hayes Arboretum Road-to-Test Road reach as 5.8 to 6 miles, Class I(IV), tied to the East Fork Whitewater River at Abington USGS gauge, with current-looking flow context. City of Richmond and Whitewater Gorge project sources confirm the public Whitewater Gorge Park corridor between Test Road and Waterfall Road, and a 2025 Test Road trailhead/kayak-launch improvement lead. No route was added because the reach is dam-risky: AW flags several likely riverwide low-head dams/weirs and says to scout/portage well upstream/downstream where boil lines are unsafe. The upstream Hayes Arboretum Road put-in was not confirmed as a modern manager-grade public paddling access, the automation shell could not reach USGS Water Services for recurring Indiana gauges, and this would be a whitewater/dam-hazard route rather than a conservative first Indiana add.
candidates.retryCondition: Retry only if a manager-grade source confirms a safe public put-in and take-out pair with coordinates, USGS 03275600 is product-fetchable in-app, and AW/manager guidance plus official low-head-dam context supports a safe route plan around every dam/weir.
